tweets.text:
While employment data appears to be improving, unit labor costs are growing at the lowest year-over-year pace in five years. 
This is a very important point for the "inflationistas." Wage growth is high correlated to inflation over time (as that is where demand derives from). and once the oil spike resolves, inflationary pressures will likely revert to the underlying economy.
tweets.full_text:
While employment data appears to be improving, unit labor costs are growing at the lowest year-over-year pace in five years. 
This is a very important point for the "inflationistas." Wage growth is high correlated to inflation over time (as that is where demand derives from). and once the oil spike resolves, inflationary pressures will likely revert to the underlying economy.

tweets.text: Revelio Labs, BofA and ADP all suggest that employment growth continued in May as the economy continues to show signs of growth. Interestingly, it appears that much of the employment growth may be centered around lower wage jobs (according to BofA). https://t.co/NTfj1txm2y
